meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategies for Founders: Ship Faster & Scale
content.seo_description: Stop stalling your SaaS. Learn how strategic pricing solves the 'can't finish' cycle. Expert guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid deployment.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of market-validated pricing. By implementing a value-based pricing model early, you force product scope to align with revenue-generating features. This creates a feedback loop that sustains momentum, kills feature creep, and ensures you build what customers actually pay for.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS startups is littered with 'perfect' code that never saw a dollar. You’ve been there: three months in, feature creep sets in, the vision blurs, and the project stalls because you’re building for an imaginary audience. The friction isn't technical—it's strategic. When your pricing model is undefined, your development roadmap becomes an infinite loop of 'nice-to-haves' that evaporate your motivation.

True velocity comes from the constraints of a paid transaction. By anchoring your development to a concrete pricing tier, you transform from a tinkerer into a founder. We’re going to use Antigravity to structure your revenue logic and Claude Code to execute the build, ensuring every line of code you write is a step toward your first MRR.

steps.title: Define Value-Based Tiers
steps.description: Identify the core pain point that justifies a recurring payment. Avoid cost-plus pricing; focus on the business impact for your user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a SaaS pricing model schema that maps three tiers (Starter, Pro, Enterprise) to specific feature sets derived from user ROI, not just resource consumption.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate pricing_model.json based on value-based SaaS methodology'

steps.title: Build the Revenue Gate
steps.description: Implement a hard gate in your application logic that prevents users from accessing core features without an active subscription.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a middleware implementation for Stripe integration that validates subscription status before executing API calls.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement stripe_middleware.ts to protect route handlers'

steps.title: Deploy the MVP Checkout
steps.description: Shorten the time between 'sign up' and 'swipe' by using a pre-built, high-conversion checkout flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a minimal UI component for a pricing table that highlights the 'Pro' tier and redirects to a Stripe checkout session.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'build pricing_table_component.tsx with stripe checkout integration'

steps.title: Automate Usage Tracking
steps.description: Connect your code to billing metrics so your product can scale automatically as the user finds value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a service layer that logs user usage events to Stripe Metered Billing API to enable usage-based pricing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add usage_tracking_service.ts to monitor feature consumption'

steps.title: Validate with Real Transactions
steps.description: Ship the pricing gate to production. If no one buys, you saved yourself months of building the wrong product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a dashboard summary component that displays conversion rates and churn metrics for the current pricing tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate analytics_dashboard.tsx for subscription metrics'

pro_tips: Always price 20% higher than your gut feeling; if you don't feel slightly uncomfortable, you are underpricing your value.
Use a 'Founding Member' discount for your first 10 users to get feedback, not just revenue.
Never build an 'Enterprise' tier until your first three 'Pro' customers ask for it specifically.
Kill any feature that doesn't map directly to a line item in your pricing model.
Use Stripe's 'Customer Portal' instead of building your own billing management UI to save 40+ hours of development.
content.next_steps: Deploy your pricing gate to a staging environment, run a test charge via Stripe's test mode, and then open the doors to your first five potential customers for validation.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in a vacuum. Your product deserves to be a business, and your time is the most valuable asset you have. Leverage these tools to cut through the noise and validate your revenue model today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
